examples.title: Software Quality Metrics Calculator
examples.description: Calculate basic quality metrics for a software project
examples.code:
PROGRAM QualityMetricsCalculator
BEGIN
 INPUT: defects, linesOfCode, testHours, failureCount, operatingTime

 // Defect Density
 defectDensity = defects / linesOfCode
 PRINT "Defect Density: " + defectDensity + " defects/KLOC"

 // Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF)
 MTBF = operatingTime / failureCount
 PRINT "MTBF: " + MTBF + " hours"

 // Availability
 MTTR = testHours / failureCount
 availability = MTBF / (MTBF + MTTR) * 100
 PRINT "Availability: " + availability + "%"

 // Test Effectiveness
 testEffectiveness = (defectsFound / totalDefects) * 100
 PRINT "Test Effectiveness: " + testEffectiveness + "%"
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates calculation of basic software quality metrics including defect density (quality of development), MTBF (reliability), availability (maintainability), and test effectiveness (QC performance).

examples.title: Quality Assessment Framework
examples.description: Framework for assessing software quality based on ISO 9126 characteristics
examples.code:
PROGRAM QualityAssessment
BEGIN
 INPUT: scores[6] // Array for 6 characteristics
 weights[6] = {0.25, 0.20, 0.15, 0.15, 0.15, 0.10}
 // Characteristics: Functionality, Reliability, Usability,
 // Efficiency, Maintainability, Portability

 PRINT "Enter scores (0-100) for each ISO 9126 characteristic:"
 FOR i = 0 TO 5 DO
 PRINT characteristicNames[i]
 INPUT scores[i]
 END FOR

 // Calculate weighted quality index
 qualityIndex = 0
 FOR i = 0 TO 5 DO
 qualityIndex = qualityIndex + (scores[i] * weights[i])
 END FOR

 PRINT "Overall Quality Index: " + qualityIndex

 IF qualityIndex >= 80 THEN
 PRINT "Quality Grade: Excellent"
 ELSE IF qualityIndex >= 60 THEN
 PRINT "Quality Grade: Good"
 ELSE IF qualityIndex >= 40 THEN
 PRINT "Quality Grade: Satisfactory"
 ELSE
 PRINT "Quality Grade: Needs Improvement"
 END IF
END
examples.explanation: This demonstrates a weighted quality assessment model where different ISO 9126 characteristics are assigned weights based on project requirements, providing an overall quality index.
